stories.title: Is PlayStation Network Down Right Now? Players Report PSN Issues
stories.summary: Thousands of players have reported that PlayStation Network is down for them on July 24. Here is everything you need to know about the current PlayStation status and whether it’s down or not. Screenshot: PlayStation At the time of writing, many players are reporting that PlayStation Network is down . So, if you are getting disconnected from PSN, you are not alone! However, according to the official PlayStation Status checker, all online services for PS4 and PS5 are currently “up and running”. So, as off now, it appears to be localized server issues and not a widespread PlayStation Network outage. This could, however, change.

stories.title: Former Corrie star Tracy Shaw shares cancer update
stories.summary: Former Coronation Street star Tracy Shaw shares cancer update Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 08:25 Getty Tracy Shaw appeared on Coronation Street from 1995 - 2003 as Maxine Peacock Former Coronation Street actress Tracy Shaw has shared an update about her breast cancer, saying surgery is not currently possible as her tumours have not shrunk in size. The actress and singer, 52, revealed she was diagnosed with the disease in April and has since shared regular updates on social media, including what she is facing during her rounds of chemotherapy.

stories.title: Weight loss drugs 'linked to hair loss' - study
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 08:54 Getty Experts used electronic patient data from the University of Pennsylvania Health System. Weight loss and diabetes drugs like Mounjaro, Ozempic and Wegovy are linked to an increased risk of hair loss, according to researchers. People on GLP-1 agonist drugs have been reporting hair loss to regulators, but a new study on around 50,000 people appears to firm up the link. Experts used electronic patient data from the University of Pennsylvania Health System to compare rates of alopecia hair loss in adults using GLP-1s to manage diabetes with those on other drugs.

stories.title: Robbie Williams adds second Limerick show
stories.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 11:04 Getty Robbie Williams will now play two dates at Limerick's Thomond Park Robbie Williams has announced a second Thomond Park Stadium concert after his first Limerick show sold out. The singer will now perform at the venue on 7 July 2027, following the sell-out of his previously announced concert on 6 July. The shows, which are subject to licence, will be his only Irish dates of 2027. Tickets for the extra show are on sale now through Ticketmaster. Williams announced earlier this week that he would bring his RW27 tour to Thomond Park for the first time. The Limerick concerts follow his sold-out headline show at Dublin's Croke Park last summer.

stories.title: Jurgen Klopp: Germany appoint former Liverpool boss as new head coach to replace Julian Nagelsmann
stories.summary: Jurgen Klopp has warned he will walk away from his new job as Germany's head coach if the German media don't "leave my family in peace". Klopp has returned to management on a contract that runs until the end of the 2030 World Cup after leaving Liverpool in 2024 because he was "running out of energy" , having won six trophies in nine years at the club. The 59-year-old was unveiled on Friday and issued a stark warning to journalists after his predecessor Julian Nagelsmann faced intense scrutiny and criticism from the media. Transfer Centre LIVE! | Latest on YOUR PL club! Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract on NOW 📺 Choose the Sky Sports push notifications you want!

stories.title: Chris Affleck, Mother of Oscar Winners Ben and Casey Affleck, Dies at 83
stories.summary: Chris Anne Affleck, the mother of Oscar winners Ben Affleck and Casey Affleck , died June 2, her family announced . She was 83 and diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in December. Christine Anne Boldt was born in New York City in December 1942. She was a graduate of Harvard University and spent 35 years as a public school teacher. She retired in 2008 and was a fierce civil rights advocate throughout her life, having served as one of the original freedom riders who went into the Deep South during the 1960s. Chris and her ex-husband Timothy Affleck welcomed their first child, Ben, in 1972.

stories.title: Ryanair broken window may have been caused by 'foreign object'
stories.summary: 3 days ago Emer Moreau Business reporter An incident on a Ryanair flight which saw a passenger partially sucked out of a plane window may have been caused by "foreign object damage" to an engine, said the airline's boss. Michael O'Leary said an initial investigation into the incident on 10 July between Greece and Germany suggests "it looks like a foreign object damage to the engine on takeoff at Thessaloniki but . we can't say that definitively". He insisted that the engine on the 18-year-old Boeing 737 plane had been fully serviced and overhauled in the past two years.

stories.title: Croatian test awaits for City
stories.summary: Derry City will face Croatian opposition for the first time in our history when we meet HNK Rijeka in the second round of UEFA Conference League qualifying on Thursday. I’ve had a look at Croatia’s third most successful team ahead of the tie. Knocked out in the Round of 16 by eventual finalists RC Strasbourg in thiscompetition last year, Rijeka are becoming an established team in UEFA competition and defeated Irish opposition in Shelbourne during qualifying last season. HNK Rijeka had two players representing them in the FIFA World Cup this summer. The first was star man and top goalscorer Toni Fruk, who was in the Croatia squad.

stories.title: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
stories.summary: In cinemas, the film's global takings include £12. 6m from the UK - making it the second-biggest opening weekend in the country so far this year, behind Toy Story 5. In the process, The Odyssey broke the record for the highest takings in an opening weekend for a film on Imax screens in the UK, according to industry publication Screen Daily , external . The three-hour epic, which stars Matt Damon, Tom Holland and Anne Hathaway, was the first theatrical release to be shot entirely with Imax 70mm film cameras. While there are more than 50 Imax screens in the UK, only three are showing it using 70mm film projectors - the BFI and Science Museum in London, and Printworks in Manchester.

stories.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
stories.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread Figure caption, Watch: Wildfires rage across France and Spain By Cachella Smith and Paul Kirby Europe digital editor Updated 2 minutes ago French authorities have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the south-west coast, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat.

stories.title: Burnham urged to lobby EU leaders directly to waive EES border controls
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is being urged to lobby EU leaders to suspend the new Entry-Exit System (EES) border controls as 2.2 million British holidaymakers prepare to travel abroad this weekend. The EES, which requires fingerprinting and photo registration for non-EU travelers, has caused long queues and missed flights since its full rollout in April. Liberal Democrat foreign affairs spokesperson Calum Miller says the system should be paused until at least September to avoid further disruptions.

stories.title: India’s Paid Music Subscribers Could Hit 30 Million by 2028, EY-IMI Report Finds
stories.summary: India ‘s paid music streaming subscriber base could grow to between 28 million and 30 million by 2028, up from an estimated 14 million as of December 2025, according to the EY and the Indian Music Industry (IMI) “How India Listens, Streams and Pays for Music” report, released Friday in Mumbai. The report draws on a poll of upward of 15,000 smartphone owners fielded in March and April, supplemented by psychometric research covering more than 2,200 consumers and discussions with industry executives. The numbers underline a familiar paradox for the Indian music business. Some 96% of smartphone owners consume music, and 80% of them listen for more than an hour a day.

stories.title: Facebook considers giving up and becoming TikTok
stories.summary: Facebook is planning some big changes to try and keep its users from jumping to rival social platforms like TikTok — changes that sound dubiously similar to becoming a TikTok clone. Facebook head Tom Alison announced today that the platform will begin testing a “reimagined experience” later this year that will put a subset of users into a “full-screen video the moment they open the app. ” Meta didn’t share examples of what its experimental new interface will look like, only that it will initially roll out to countries with a noted preference for video content, with a potential expansion planned for the US next year.

stories.title: AI Has Changed the Rules in the Fight Against Fraud -
stories.summary: Weightmans partner and Head of Fraud Mike Brown reflects on how AI isn’t just enabling fraud on a greater scale, it’s beginning to normalise it, creating a challenge for insurers and businesses that extends far beyond technology and into consumer behaviour. Cast your mind back – and be honest. We’ve all been there at some point in the dim and distant past. Sat in the nurse’s office at school, clutching ourselves and turning a mild twinge into a crippling pain. Presenting a playground graze of the knee as an agonising wound and pleading with the school secretary to call our parents to take us home.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Burnham told the assembled civil servants "It's not just a new office - through this place power is going to flow, energy is going to flow. " He added: "It's sending a message to everyone here that we've got support of the government from the very top - it's a very powerful message. The political commitment is there. " Other ministers due for meetings at the Manchester hub on Friday include Angela Rayner, Pat McFadden, Jonathan Reynolds, Yvette Cooper, Angela Eagle, Heidi Alexander and Miatta Fahnbulleh.

stories.title: Kino Rebelde Boards Locarno Title ‘September Afternoon’ for International Sales (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Kino Rebelde has picked up international sales rights for “September Afternoon” by writer-director Nicolaas Schmidt , which has its world premiere in the Cineasti del Presente competition section at the Locarno Film Festival . The film is described as a work that “transforms the romantic idea of the summer retreat into a quiet meditation on contemporary resignation, fragile intimacy, and the longing for connection. ” In the film, a couple refuses to accept that summer is over. For a last time, they head to the coast with the intention of spending two afternoons together between the sand dunes and the sea.

stories.title: GB Paralympian Schrager given two-year ban after failed drug test
stories.summary: Daphne Schrager, Paralympic silver medallist from Paris 2024, has been banned for two years after testing positive for the muscle-building substance Ligandrol. The ban, backdated to June 2025, ends in June 2027, allowing her to compete at the 2028 Paralympics. She has been excluded from England’s Commonwealth Games squad in Glasgow.

stories.title: Preston Bus still to decide on Burnham's £2 cap
stories.summary: The operator says it needs more details before deciding whether to join Andy Burnham's new scheme. Preston Bus says it cannot confirm whether it will join a new government scheme capping single fares at £2 from January because it has not been told how operators will be reimbursed. Under the current national scheme, participating operators cap eligible single fares at £3, but participation is voluntary. Preston Bus left the scheme in January and now charges £3. 50 for a standard adult single. The government said the new scheme will run throughout 2027 and is backed by £400m, but its announcement does not set out payments for individual operators.

stories.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
stories.summary: A South African court has temporarily blocked parliament’s impeachment inquiry into President Cyril Ramaphosa over the "Farmgate" cash-in-sofa scandal. The Constitutional Court’s May ruling forced lawmakers to revive the probe after they initially shelved it. Ramaphosa denies wrongdoing and is separately challenging the panel’s findings in court next month.

stories.title: Devlin opens up on 'too stressful' Hearts exit for Rangers
stories.summary: Cammy Devlin says it was "too stressful" to head to the World Cup with his club future still to be decided - although the extra time taken did lead to a move to Rangers. "It was devastating, but I didn't have much time to reflect on how last season ended and then straight into the World Cup, which is one of the most special moments of your career. "My mum, my dad and my girlfriend are over in America supporting you. So you are trying to enjoy that as much as you can. "I thought it would be a good idea going there off-contract, but it wasn't. "It was too stressful.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: Since the resumption of hostilities between the US and Iran, wholesale oil prices have surged above $100 per barrel, leading to a significant rise in UK fuel prices, which are now at their highest level since mid-June. This increase underscores how global geopolitical tensions directly impact local consumer costs.

stories.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
stories.summary: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. Jurgen Klopp started his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. The former Liverpool manager was appointed on a four-year deal on Friday to replace Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned this month after Germany's shock World Cup defeat by Paraguay in the last 32. But the 59-year-old issued a warning to the media as he held his first press conference as Germany's head coach. "If you behave badly and don't leave my family in peace then I'll be gone, I'll just turn away," Klopp said.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
stories.summary: The meeting came hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Thousands of people continue to protest demanding the Indian education minister's resignation after representatives of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) held talks with the government. CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka told reporters that the government has asked for a day to decide on their biggest demand - minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. "The protest will continue and will keep growing until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns. If that does not happen soon, we will be forced to announce another nationwide protest call," said Ranka.

stories.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
stories.summary: Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.

stories.title: Russia and Ukraine Trade Blows With Missiles and Drones
stories.summary: Ukrainian strikes set fire to warehouses and other sites in Russia, and Russian bombing rocked Ukraine’s capital, as the two countries escalated a deadly air campaign.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. Iran says it has carried out drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East in retaliation for the 13th consecutive night of American attacks on the country. Explosions were reported along Iran's Gulf coast, and in western, northern and central areas. Four people were killed and five injured in an attack in the south-west, Iranian state media said. Iran later announced strikes on US-allied Kuwait, while sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights were suspended at a major Iraqi airport after a reported drone crash.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman’s wedding planning triggered a body-loathing spiral, sending her into extreme calorie counting and bingeing. She lost years of healthy habits after tracking weight daily and restricting food, despite recognizing the unhealthy shift.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

offbeat.title: Is PlayStation Network Down Right Now? Players Report PSN Issues
offbeat.summary: Thousands of players have reported that PlayStation Network is down for them on July 24. Here is everything you need to know about the current PlayStation status and whether it’s down or not. Screenshot: PlayStation At the time of writing, many players are reporting that PlayStation Network is down . So, if you are getting disconnected from PSN, you are not alone! However, according to the official PlayStation Status checker, all online services for PS4 and PS5 are currently “up and running”. So, as off now, it appears to be localized server issues and not a widespread PlayStation Network outage. This could, however, change.

sports.summary: Cammy Devlin says it was "too stressful" to head to the World Cup with his club future still to be decided - although the extra time taken did lead to a move to Rangers. "It was devastating, but I didn't have much time to reflect on how last season ended and then straight into the World Cup, which is one of the most special moments of your career. "My mum, my dad and my girlfriend are over in America supporting you. So you are trying to enjoy that as much as you can. "I thought it would be a good idea going there off-contract, but it wasn't. "It was too stressful.

sports.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
sports.summary: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. Jurgen Klopp started his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. The former Liverpool manager was appointed on a four-year deal on Friday to replace Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned this month after Germany's shock World Cup defeat by Paraguay in the last 32. But the 59-year-old issued a warning to the media as he held his first press conference as Germany's head coach. "If you behave badly and don't leave my family in peace then I'll be gone, I'll just turn away," Klopp said.

crisis.title: Preston Bus still to decide on Burnham's £2 cap
crisis.summary: The operator says it needs more details before deciding whether to join Andy Burnham's new scheme. Preston Bus says it cannot confirm whether it will join a new government scheme capping single fares at £2 from January because it has not been told how operators will be reimbursed. Under the current national scheme, participating operators cap eligible single fares at £3, but participation is voluntary. Preston Bus left the scheme in January and now charges £3. 50 for a standard adult single. The government said the new scheme will run throughout 2027 and is backed by £400m, but its announcement does not set out payments for individual operators.

crisis.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
crisis.summary: Since the resumption of hostilities between the US and Iran, wholesale oil prices have surged above $100 per barrel, leading to a significant rise in UK fuel prices, which are now at their highest level since mid-June. This increase underscores how global geopolitical tensions directly impact local consumer costs.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.

health.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
health.summary: A woman’s wedding planning triggered a body-loathing spiral, sending her into extreme calorie counting and bingeing. She lost years of healthy habits after tracking weight daily and restricting food, despite recognizing the unhealthy shift.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.
